executiveSummary:
# Medicaid Program Executive Summary - February 2026

National Medicaid enrollment has reached 74.9 million beneficiaries as of February 2026, with all major states experiencing year-over-year declines following the end of pandemic-era continuous enrollment protections. California leads with 12.4 million enrollees despite a significant 6.8% decrease, while Texas maintains 4.0 million enrollees with a more moderate 3.8% decline. Michigan experienced the steepest enrollment drop at 7.6%, while North Carolina uniquely held enrollment flat. Total national Medicaid expenditures for fiscal year 2024 reached $215.9 billion, with a national average per-enrollee cost of $2,883. Notably, spending patterns vary dramatically across states, with Massachusetts ($4,221 per enrollee) and Pennsylvania ($4,363 per enrollee) demonstrating costs nearly double the national average, while Florida operates at just $2,036 per enrollee.

Texas presents a mixed performance profile compared to peer states. While the state's per-enrollee cost of $3,014 remains close to the national average and below high-cost states like New York ($3,460), Texas significantly trails in quality measures with a score of 62 out of 100, ranking 30th nationally in the "moderate" tier. This contrasts unfavorably with top-performing states like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80), and even falls below the national average of 65.6. However, Texas demonstrates strong managed care implementation with 93.3% penetration, exceeding the national average of 83.2%. Recent federal regulatory activity indicates heightened CMS oversight with multiple provider enrollment moratoria and enhanced scrutiny of state-directed payments, requiring careful monitoring of compliance implications for high-volume states like Texas as the program continues normalizing post-pandemic operations.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas's Medicaid program demonstrates mixed performance with notable operational strengths but persistent quality challenges. With 4.0 million enrollees, Texas ranks third nationally behind California and New York, though its enrollment declined 3.8% year-over-year—roughly in line with the national trend as states completed post-pandemic eligibility redeterminations. At $3,014 per enrollee, Texas spends 4.5% above the national average of $2,883, positioning it between cost-efficient southern peers like Florida ($2,036) and North Carolina ($2,623), while remaining well below expansion states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Ohio ($3,584). The state's managed care penetration rate of 93.3% significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong infrastructure for care coordination and cost management that likely contributes to its relatively moderate per-enrollee spending despite serving a large, diverse population.

However, Texas faces substantial quality improvement challenges that warrant close monitoring. The state's quality score of 62/100 ranks 30th nationally and places it in the bottom tier among large states, trailing peers like North Carolina and falling short of high-performing states by nearly 20 points. While Texas avoids the bottom quintile occupied by other southern non-expansion states like Alabama (52) and Mississippi (48), its quality performance lags behind what would be expected given its operational scale and managed care sophistication. Key areas to watch include whether recent federal regulatory activity around state-directed payments and provider enrollment moratoria will impact Texas's managed care model effectiveness, and whether the state can leverage its strong managed care penetration to drive quality improvements. The convergence of cost efficiency and quality enhancement will be critical as Texas navigates ongoing federal oversight while serving one of the nation's largest and most complex Medicaid populations.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 51% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost inefficiencies.

alerts.title: Florida Per-Enrollee Spending 29% Below National Average
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,036 is notably lower than the national average of $2,883, suggesting potential cost efficiencies or service gaps.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critical Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi have quality scores below 55, significantly underperforming the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Mass. High Spending with Exceptional Quality Returns
alerts.description: Massachusetts combines high per-enrollee spending ($4,221) with the nation's top quality score (81), demonstrating effective resource utilization.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid enrollment drops 3.8% YoY amid national decline trends
executiveInsights.summary: Texas lost ~152k Medicaid enrollees with 3.8% YoY decline, tracking below national decline rate. State remains 3rd largest program with nearly 4M enrollees.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment declines directly impact contract value and may signal post-PHE unwinding challenges requiring operational adjustments and member retention strategies.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review enrollment trend drivers with HHSC leadership this week

executiveInsights.title: CMS announces nationwide provider enrollment moratoriums affecting Texas
executiveInsights.summary: Two Federal Register notices announce temporary moratoriums on Medicare/Medicaid provider enrollment. These nationwide policies will directly impact Texas Medicaid operations.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider network adequacy and access issues could emerge, requiring immediate coordination with HHSC on network management and compliance strategies.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag provider enrollment moratorium impacts to HHSC compliance team

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score of 62/100 ranks #30 nationally, below national average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scores 62/100 vs national average of 65.6, placing it in moderate tier. Only 10 states achieve high performer status (≥75).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Quality performance directly impacts federal funding, contract bonuses, and regulatory scrutiny, requiring targeted improvement initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review quality improvement roadmap priorities with HHSC quality team

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs of $3,014 exceed national average by $131
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per enrollee vs national average of $2,883, representing 4.5% premium. Total Texas expenditures reach $12B annually.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-member costs may indicate care management opportunities or cost containment challenges that could affect contract economics and state budget pressures.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost driver analysis and benchmarking with HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: New CMS rule on managed care state directed payments published
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register published new Medicaid managed care state directed payments rule affecting fee-for-service arrangements. Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: With Texas's high managed care penetration, payment methodology changes could significantly impact contract financial arrangements and compliance requirements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review new payment rule implications for Texas managed care contracts

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline acceleration
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropping 3.8% YoY amid national unwinding may trigger budget pressures and system modernization needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose enrollment stabilization analytics and retention tools to HHSC.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality performance gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, creating vulnerability for federal oversight and managed care contract renewals.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive quality improvement roadmap targeting top-tier performance.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: High 93.3% managed care penetration positions Texas for major MCO contract renewals worth billions in administrative oversight.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position for managed care oversight and evaluation contract opportunities.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal payment regulation
riskOpportunity.description: New CMS state directed payment rules may require significant system changes and compliance monitoring across managed care plans.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Build regulatory compliance advisory capabilities for payment rule implementation.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider enrollment moratorium
riskOpportunity.description: Nationwide temporary provider enrollment restrictions create network adequacy challenges requiring enhanced monitoring and compliance systems.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop provider network adequacy monitoring tools for HHSC compliance.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee costs at $3,014 near national average create opportunities for cost reduction initiatives and value-based analytics.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose predictive analytics platform for cost management and fraud detection.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG oversight
riskOpportunity.description: Active federal oversight with expanding work plans increases audit risk but creates demand for compliance and monitoring services.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen compliance monitoring and audit support service offerings.

riskOpportunity.label: Digital transformation lag
riskOpportunity.description: Moderate quality performance suggests potential gaps in digital infrastructure requiring modernization investments and system integration.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Target upcoming technology modernization RFPs through ESBD monitoring.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas Medicaid enrollment decreased by 3.8% year-over-year to 3,997,752 enrollees as of February 2026.

pulseMetrics.label: 12-Mo Enrollment Delta
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas enrollment declined 3.8% year-over-year, slightly better than the national trend.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee annually, above the national average of $2,883.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 enrollees, well above the national average of 83.2%.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas ranks #30 of 50 states with a quality score of 62, in the moderate tier below the national average of 65.6.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two Texas procurement opportunities are tracked through HHSC portal and ESBD systems.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Ten federal regulatory items are being monitored across HHS OIG oversight and CMS rulemaking activities.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific upcoming milestones are identified in the current data.
